summary: Iteration 4 (post-refactor smoke retest) PASSED on all 6 required scenarios. The pages/ → views/ rename is clean: sidebar shows exactly the 8 curated entries (Dashboard, Stock Details, Compare, News, Portfolio, Watchlist, AI Chatbot, Account) with NO auto-discovered duplicates (no 'home' / 'stock_details' / 'chatbot' lowercase/underscored entries). Portfolio Buy 10 AAPL renders Holdings/Allocation/Equity Curve with 0 stException + no Traceback. Watchlist add NVDA then TCS.NS → .section-h reads '2 Tickers' and Manage watchlist expander shows exactly 2 Remove buttons. AI Chatbot fallback panel correctly shows 'Gemini API key not configured' + the aistudio.google.com/apikey URL with 0 stException. News page shows 'NewsAPI key not configured' panel. Full 6-page smoke (Dashboard / Stock Details / Compare / News / AI Chatbot / Account) has 0 stException across the board. Logout cleanup works: sidebar reverts to '👋 Log in to unlock portfolio & watchlist' caption and /Portfolio shows 'Please log in to use your portfolio'.

validated_flows.precondition_app_loads_and_no_duplicate_: PASS - http://localhost:8501/ renders with 'Market Overview' heading, 0 stException, no 'Page not found' modal. Sidebar links = ['📊Dashboard','🔎Stock Details','📐Compare','📰News','💼Portfolio','⭐Watchlist','🤖AI Chatbot','🔐Account']. No auto-discovered entries (the views/ rename successfully suppresses Streamlit's pages/ auto-discovery).
validated_flows.regression_guard_A_portfolio_equity_curv: PASS - Signed up qa_1782717171/qa_1782717171@test.com/test1234, logged in, navigated to Portfolio, bought 10 AAPL (default Live price). After ~13s: 0 stException, no 'Traceback' text, AND one of Holdings/Allocation/Equity Curve is visible.
validated_flows.regression_guard_B_watchlist_double_add: PASS - After adding NVDA (9s wait) and TCS.NS (11s wait), .section-h.first.text_content() = '2 Tickers' (case-insensitive match contains '2 tickers'). Manage watchlist expander shows exactly 2 Remove buttons.
validated_flows.ai_chatbot_key_missing_fallback: PASS - Chatbot page shows yellow warning containing 'Gemini API key not configured' AND the aistudio.google.com/apikey URL. 0 stException. (Multi-conversation UI correctly hidden behind ai.is_configured() check — deferred to a future iteration with a real key.)
validated_flows.full_pages_smoke_after_rename: PASS - Dashboard / Stock Details (AAPL renders) / Compare / News / AI Chatbot / Account all render with 0 stException. News shows NewsAPI 'key not configured' panel.
validated_flows.logout_cleanup: PASS - Clicking sidebar Log out (scoped via stSidebarUserContent) reverts sidebar to '👋 Log in to unlock portfolio & watchlist' caption, and navigating to Portfolio shows 'Please log in to use your portfolio.' with 0 stException.

critical_code_review_comments: Account page renders its own 'Log out' button in the main area in addition to the sidebar 'Log out' button while logged in (visible at AI Stock Dashboard welcome view). This creates a strict-mode selector collision for tests that use get_by_role('button', name='Log out') unscoped. Not a product bug — both buttons work and call the same handler. Future-proofing: consider giving the buttons distinct aria-labels (e.g., 'Log out from sidebar' vs 'Log out from account page') or using key='sidebar_logout' / key='account_logout' so tests can disambiguate cleanly. Test scripts in iteration 5+ should use page.get_by_test_id('stSidebarUserContent').get_by_role('button', name='Log out') when targeting the sidebar logout.

Multi-conversation chatbot UI (left column history + new chat / delete / rename, right column messages) is correctly gated behind ai.is_configured(), so without GOOGLE_API_KEY the new SQLite-backed conversations/chat_messages persistence layer cannot be exercised end-to-end at the UI layer. Main agent has reported this CRUD layer was already verified directly via Python; recommend a deferred UI-level retest once a Gemini key is available.

The two new tables (conversations, chat_messages) were not directly inspected this iteration since the chatbot UI is behind the key check. Trusting the main agent's note that schema is present and CRUD verified.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (6/6 required flows PASS; 0 stException across the entire session)

test_credentials: qa_1782717171 / qa_1782717171@test.com / test1234 (created this iteration). Additional users qa_1782717377/... created during logout-flow retest. Previous-iteration users still present in SQLite from earlier runs.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 new users (qa_1782717171 and qa_1782717377). For qa_1782717171: bought 10 AAPL in portfolio; added NVDA and TCS.NS to watchlist (both remain). For qa_1782717377: used only for logout-flow verification, no holdings/watchlist data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: App at http://localhost:8501/ is healthy after the pages/ → views/ rename. Sidebar contract: 8 curated st.Page entries (Dashboard at '/', plus url_path slugs stock / compare / news / portfolio / watchlist / chat / account). Use page.get_by_role('link', name='X').click() for sidebar nav. Auth selectors unchanged from iter 3 (Sign-up tab: Username / Email / Password (min 6 chars) / Confirm password / 'Create account'; Log-in tab: 'Email or username' / 'Password' / 'Log in'). For Log out, the Account page also renders a 'Log out' button in the main content area, causing a strict-mode collision; ALWAYS scope sidebar logout via page.get_by_test_id('stSidebarUserContent').get_by_role('button', name='Log out'). Watchlist count: read page.locator('.section-h').first.text_content() and compare case-insensitively (CSS uppercases the text). Allow 9-11s after Watchlist Add, 13s after Portfolio Buy. The chatbot's multi-conversation UI is behind ai.is_configured() — it cannot be UI-tested without a GOOGLE_API_KEY in environment.
rca_of_the_issue: Not applicable — all 6 required scenarios passed on first attempt. Only a test-scripting strict-mode collision was observed for the unscoped 'Log out' button (because Account page renders its own Log out button in addition to the sidebar one); resolved by scoping the locator to stSidebarUserContent. This is a test-selector concern, not a product bug.
